terms.definition: The capacity of a system’s parts to operate in mutually reinforcing alignment without destructive interference.
A scalar index (0–1) quantifying systemic coherence; higher values denote tighter internal alignment.
The empirically observed χ value (~0.35) below which a system loses self-corrective capacity.
The Coherence Factor applied to an individual across micro-domains (family, trust, etc.).
An exogenous source of ordering energy posited to raise coherence when internal resources fail.
The intentional lowering of ego-barriers that allows the β coefficient to couple into the system.
A stepwise procedure (exemplified by AA’s Twelve Steps) that systematically raises χ via β activation.
